article_meta.title: The three-way battle between Democrats running for Senate in Illinois has evolved into an argument about the best way to

reasoning: The summary maintains neutral language, attributes positions to candidates with quotes where appropriate, and avoids adopting loaded framing or subjective adjectives. It balances perspectives presented in the article without inserting editorial tone or sensationalism.

custom_reasoning.spin: Uses neutral verbs and attributions; no charged phrasing like ‘rammed through’ or ‘brutal.’
custom_reasoning.unsubstantiated_claims: Claims are tied to article content (e.g., Mejia’s win) and framed as suggestions, not certainties.
custom_reasoning.opinion_as_fact: Evaluative points (political risk) are attributed to ‘some Democrats.’
custom_reasoning.sensationalism: No heightened emotional language; tone is measured.
custom_reasoning.mudslinging: No character attacks or derogatory labels.
custom_reasoning.mind_reading: No assertions about candidates’ intentions beyond stated positions.
custom_reasoning.slant: Summarizes each candidate’s stance and notes intra-party concerns, matching article balance.
custom_reasoning.bias_by_omission: Includes major perspectives present: the three candidates’ views and broader Democratic concerns.
custom_reasoning.subjective_adjectives: Avoids subjective descriptors; sticks to factual wording.
custom_reasoning.word_choice: Politically neutral terminology; quoted slogans are clearly marked.
custom_reasoning.negativity_bias: Does not overemphasize negative outcomes beyond article’s content.
custom_reasoning.elite_populist_bias: No preferential weighting of elites vs. grassroots; mirrors article sourcing.
custom_reasoning.framing_neutrality: More neutral paraphrase; avoids adopting any loaded framing as fact.
custom_reasoning.perspective_completeness: Captures all distinct perspectives included in the source article.
